description: Routing manifest for the Design Fleet skills. Read by the bundled `design-fleet route` preview command. Real agents in Cursor and Claude Code do not read this file at runtime — they pick skills based on each SKILL.md description. Treat the routing_notes section below as guidance, not enforcement.

design-contract.description: Own the project's design truth in five modes: setup runs the taste questionnaire and writes design-taste.mdc; assess rates codebase design consistency; draft generates a filled VISION.md hard-rails contract; reconcile diffs the doc against reality; plan turns FEEDBACK.md and the north star into proposed Goals rows

setup.precondition: design-taste.mdc does not exist (or user wants to regenerate)

not_this_skill.when: user wants a visual critique, Nielsen heuristic review, or to verify just-generated code
taste profile already exists and user only wants to review it

frontend-design.description: Build distinctive, production-grade UI — components, pages, layouts — honoring the project's VISION.md contract when present, and owning motion: easing, timing, springs, exit animations, reduced motion, plus advanced patterns (pseudo-elements, View Transitions, morphing icons, container animation, predictive prefetching)

not_this_skill.when: user wants to review existing UI quality, run a Nielsen heuristic review, or verify the just-built code follows the rules
user wants a codebase audit, a project VISION.md, or a taste profile

design-review.description: Two-altitude evaluator of existing UI. Deterministic floor (formerly design-lint): validates code against grep-able check IDs and the VISION.md contract via `npx design-fleet lint`, producing a pass/fail report. Subjective ceiling (formerly design-critique): a systematic visual + behavioral critique of a screenshot, file, or URL, with a formal Nielsen Heuristic Mode on explicit request
triggers: did I follow the design rules
does this pass the design rules
design-review.output_notes: Deterministic floor: when VISION.md exists, `npx design-fleet lint` runs the P-checks against the contract's tables; the judgment half (layout conformance, signature coverage, selection semantics) is reported under Category 8 — it BLOCKS (exit 1). Subjective ceiling: default mode emits the 7-section structured critique; explicit Nielsen/heuristic/usability-audit requests emit the 10-heuristic checklist with cosmetic/minor/major/catastrophic ratings (the two formats never mix). The subjective score is ADVISORY — it does not gate.
design-review.precondition: code or a visual artifact exists to review

not_this_skill.when: user wants to build new UI from scratch (or a motion/pattern implementation)
user wants a full codebase audit, to draft/reconcile VISION.md, or to set up a taste profile

routing_notes.advisory: This section is documentation, not runtime enforcement. The bundled `design-fleet route` command applies trigger-based scoring with a confidence threshold (>= 2.0) and falls back to the skill named in `fallback` below. Real agents in Cursor and Claude Code select skills from each SKILL.md description.
priority_order: If design-taste.mdc is missing and user is starting UI work → design-contract (setup mode)
If input is a screenshot or image → design-review
If request mentions Nielsen, heuristics, or formal usability → design-review (Nielsen Heuristic Mode)
If request is to audit/scan the codebase, draft/reconcile the VISION.md contract, or set up a taste profile → design-contract
If request is to lint, validate, or verify generated code against design rules or the contract → design-review
If request is to critique, review, or improve a specific visual artifact → design-review
Otherwise (build UI, or a motion/easing/pattern question) → frontend-design (the fallback)
routing_notes.composable: Skills can chain. Greenfield: design-contract (setup) → frontend-design → design-review. Existing project: design-contract (assess) → design-contract (draft VISION.md) → frontend-design → design-review. Maintenance: design-contract (reconcile) when the contract drifts from reality.
